name: ai-differential-list
title: AI Differential List
description: Candidate considerations for human review, with supporting and contradicting evidence, model-reported likelihood, a separate red-flag slot, and suggested information to gather.

props.name: considerations
props.description: { id, label, likelihood?, supporting?, contradicting?, gather?, note? }. Listed in the caller's order; the order is presentational, not a severity ranking.

props.name: redFlags
props.description: Rendered above and outside the list — pass AiEscalationAlert nodes. Critical possibilities must never be encoded as a ranked row.

props.name: uncertainty
props.description: What the model could not weigh — which sources it read, and which it did not.

props.name: informationToGather
props.description: Suggested information that would narrow the list, across all considerations.

props.name: showLikelihood
props.description: Set false when the caller has no calibrated number to show. Preferred over inventing one.

props.name: defaultExpanded
props.description: Consideration ids expanded on first render.

props.name: limitations
props.description: Overrides the default limitations line passed to AiAnswerCard.

clinicalNotes: A differential is a thinking aid, not a diagnosis, and the failure mode of a ranked list is that readers treat rank as danger. A high-likelihood consideration is usually the benign one; the item that kills the patient often sits fourth. The component is built around that inversion. Rank is rendered as a plain grey position marker, never as a severity colour, and the limitations line says outright that the order is not a ranking of danger. `redFlags` is a separate slot rendered above the list and outside it, intended for AiEscalationAlert: a red flag that a low likelihood could bury is not a red flag. Likelihood, when shown, is a model-reported number — the bar is neutral-coloured, is always labelled "model-reported", and is optional precisely so that a caller with no calibrated number can omit it rather than fabricate one. Every consideration carries a `contradicting` slot beside its `supporting` slot, because a list that only shows confirming evidence trains confirmation bias; `gather` names what would actually discriminate between the rows, which is the clinically useful output. Nothing here is a diagnosis, an order, or a disposition, and no action in the footer writes to the record.
notes: Composes AiAnswerCard for the shell, collapsible for per-row evidence, and progress for the likelihood bar. Defers red flags to ai-escalation-alert rather than modelling severity itself.
